keyEvents.event: Division of Korea at the 38th parallel
keyEvents.impact: Soviet-backed state founded under Kim Il-sung; the divided peninsula defines all strategy
Devastating US bombing seared anti-American siege mentality into regime ideology; war never formally ended
Self-reliance doctrine justified autarky, militarization, and the Kim personality cult

keyEvents.event: Death of Kim Il-sung and dynastic succession
keyEvents.impact: First hereditary communist succession; regime survival became synonymous with Kim family rule
Hundreds of thousands died after Soviet subsidies ended; regime learned it can survive total economic collapse
Crossed the nuclear threshold; deterrent became the regime's ultimate insurance against invasion
Third-generation rule consolidated through purges, including execution of Jang Song-thaek
Demonstrated ability to threaten the US mainland; brinkmanship followed by summit diplomacy

history.narrative: The Kim dynasty values regime survival above all else, having concluded from Iraq and Libya that only a nuclear deterrent guarantees it. Brinkmanship is a deliberate negotiating tool: manufacture crisis, extract concessions, repeat. Dependent on China and increasingly Russia, but trusts no one and isolates its population to maintain absolute control.

redLines.description: Any credible attempt at regime change or fomenting internal uprising
Decapitation strike or military action against the Kim leadership
Preemptive strike on nuclear facilities or forced denuclearization
Total blockade including Chinese border trade lifeline

internalDivisions.name: Elite loyalty fragility beneath surface conformity
Pyongyang privileged class versus deprived provinces

goals.description: Ensure survival of the Kim regime against all internal and external threats
Expand and leverage the nuclear arsenal to deter the US and extract concessions
Obtain sanctions relief and foreign currency without surrendering the nuclear program
Maintain long-term claim to the whole peninsula and undermine the South's alliance with the US
